en: The following is a transcription of Josee Yvon’s performance at the Ultimatum Festival I, Saturday, May 4th at the Foufounes Electriques. She performs excerpts from her prose-poetry publication Maitresse-Cherokees, originally edited by VLB and published in 1986. A revised edition by editors Les Herbes Rouges was reprinted in 2021. In Alan Lord’s memoir High Friends in Low Places (2022), he describes Josee Yvon and partner Denis Vanier as "the prince and princess of Quebec’s counterculture," and recounts that, in this performance, "Josee came on as a surprisingly lightly stoned poet queen, in a lovely pink strap top and jeans, and read a disjointed text too difficult to translate" (262, 130). Her work is often concerned with society's most marginalized groups, such as drug users, sex workers, and transgender individuals.

body.value: Oh ben, ca va qu’un extrait, oh ben je [unintelligible].
Ca va etre un extrait de Maitresse-Cherokees qui est mon livre qui sort chez VLB.
J’ai melange mes feuilles mais ce n’est pas tellement l’importance.
Mois j’suis un peu plus grande que Pauline.
Aye Marie il n’y a pas des feuilles dans mon [unintelligible]?
Ben voici ca c’est un texte qui s’appelle Maitresse-Cherokees, VLB editeurs. Pis la ben il faut que j’attaque la. Tu ma mis ca un peu haut peut etre.
Et c’est la feuille que j’ai perdu la, mais en tout cas.
[Giggles] Je vais prendre du [unintelligible] ca. Euh, dans ceux memes [inaudible]. Euh, ca fait deux ans. Mais ma viser par [unintelligible] la.
Ca va etre tout pour le background.
En fait [unintelligible] vingt minutes, moi la.
Mais [unintelligible] la derniere page, [unintelligible] page quatre, mais la dernier page est pas pire.
Room noise [sounds of paper flipping, distant chatter].
